data.description:
## Context (parking lot — undesigned; do NOT dispatch)

The project auto-lander (epic f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af) is deliberately **Gerrit-specific, project-owned
infrastructure** — its agent-facing landing command lives in project tooling, NOT `rebar` core,
because landing/merge-queueing is platform-specific and not part of rebar's platform-agnostic
mission.

rebar's roadmap includes **code-review support on broader platforms** (GitHub without Gerrit,
GitLab, …). IF/when a **second** platform needs to land changes, generalize the *portable* part of
the auto-lander into a **provider-abstracted landing seam in `rebar` core**:

- A platform-neutral **`land → typed outcome` interface**, where the outcome enum
  (`merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed`) is the platform-agnostic core.
- **Adapters** per platform own the status-reading + land-request specifics: Gerrit
  (votes/labels/`POST rebase`+submit), GitHub (checks + `mergeable_state` + merge queue), GitLab
  (`detailed_merge_status` + merge trains).
- The project auto-lander (f1fa) would refactor onto the Gerrit adapter with no behaviour change.

This is consistent with rebar's existing **provider-neutral direction** (identity + reconciler:
jira-accountId now, GitHub node-id later).

## Why this is an `idea`, not `open`

**YAGNI / de-frankenstein discipline.** There is **no second platform that needs to land yet**, so
building the abstraction now would be a speculative seam abstracted from a single implementation —
exactly the premature-generalization rebar avoids (cf. the identity epic cutting the keyless/Sigstore
seam until a real need existed). A Gerrit-shaped `rebar land` in core *today* is the premature
version of this. **Promote to `open` only when a concrete non-Gerrit landing requirement appears**
(likely alongside a broader-platform code-review/sync epic), at which point the two real
implementations justify the abstraction.

## Rough acceptance (when promoted — to be designed then)

- [ ] A provider-neutral landing interface in rebar defining the portable typed-outcome contract.
- [ ] At least two adapters (Gerrit + one of GitHub/GitLab) implementing land-request + status-read.
- [ ] The project auto-lander (f1fa) refactored onto the Gerrit adapter with no behaviour change.
- [ ] Driven by a real second-platform need — not built speculatively.
data.title: Platform-agnostic landing provider-seam in rebar core (Gerrit/GitHub/GitLab adapters + portable outcome enum)
